MATHEMATICS

Time allowed:3 hours
Maximum Marks:80

General Instructions :

Read the following instructions very carefully and strictly follow them:

  1. This Question paper contains 38 questions. All questions are compulsory.
  2. Question paper is divided into FIVE Sections - Section A, B, C, D and E.
  3. In Section A - Question Number 1 to 18 are Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs) and Question Number 19 and 20 are Assertion-Reason based questions of 1 mark each.
  4. In Section B - Question Number 21 to 25 are Very Short Answer (VSA) type questions, carrying 2 marks each.
  5. In Section C - Question Number 26 to 31 are Short Answer (SA) type questions, carrying 3 marks each.
  6. In Section D - Question Number 32 to 35 are Long Answer (LA) type questions, carrying 5 marks each.
  7. In Section E - Question Number 36 to 38 are case study based questions, carrying 4 marks each.
  8. There is no overall choice. However, an internal choice has been provided in 2 questions in Section - B, 3 questions in Section - C, 2 questions in Section - {tex}\boldsymbol{D}{/tex} and {tex}{2}{/tex} questions in Section - {tex}\boldsymbol{E}{/tex}.
  9. Use of calculator is not allowed.

Section - B

This section comprises Very Short Answer (VSA) type questions of 2 marks each.
21.

Evaluate: {tex}\sin ^{-1}\left(\sin \frac{13 \pi}{6}\right){/tex}{tex}+\cos ^{-1}\left(\cos \frac{\pi}{3}\right)+\tan ^{-1}(\sqrt{3}){/tex}

Solution:

{tex} \sin ^{-1}\left(\sin \frac{13 \pi}{6}\right){/tex}{tex}+\cos ^{-1}\left(\cos \frac{\pi}{3}\right)+\tan ^{-1}(\sqrt{3}) {/tex}
{tex} =\frac{\pi}{6}+\frac{\pi}{3}+\frac{\pi}{3} {/tex}
{tex} =\frac{5 \pi}{6} {/tex}

 2
22.

Given that {tex}f(x)=\frac{\log x}{x}{/tex}, find the point of local maximum of {tex}f(x){/tex}.

Solution:

{tex}{f}^{\prime}({x})=\frac{1-\log {x}}{{x}^{2}},{/tex}
{tex} \therefore {f}^{\prime}({x})=0 \Rightarrow \log {x}=1 \Rightarrow {x}={e}{/tex}
{tex}f^{\prime \prime}(x)=\frac{2 x \log x-3 x}{x^{4}} \Rightarrow f^{\prime \prime}(e)=-\frac{1}{e^{3}} \lt 0{/tex} i.e. {tex}x=e{/tex} is a point of local maximum.

 2
23.(i)

Find: {tex}\int \frac{x^{3}-1}{x^{3}-x} d x{/tex}

Solution:

{tex} \int \frac{x^3-1}{x^3-x} d x =\int\left(1+\frac{1}{x}-\frac{1}{x+1}\right) d x {/tex}
{tex} =x+\log |x|-\log |x+1|+c {/tex}

 2

OR
(ii)

Evaluate: {tex}\int_{-4}^{0}|x+2| d x{/tex}

Solution:

{tex}\int_{-4}^{0} |{x}+2 | {dx}=\int_{-4}^{-2}-({x}+2) {dx}+\int_{-2}^{0}({x}+2) {dx}{/tex}
{tex}\left.\left.=-\frac{(x+2)^{2}}{2}\right]_{-4}^{-2}+\frac{(x+2)^{2}}{2}\right]_{-2}^{0}=2+2=4{/tex}

24.(i)

If {tex}y=\left(\sin ^{-1} x\right)^{2}{/tex}, then find {tex}\left(1-x^{2}\right) \frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}-x \frac{d y}{d x}{/tex}.

Solution:

{tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{2 \sin ^{-1} x}{\sqrt{1-x^2}} \Rightarrow\left(1-x^2\right)\left(\frac{d y}{d x}\right)^2=4 y{/tex}
Differentiating with respect to '{tex}x{/tex}', we get
{tex} \left(1-x^2\right) 2 \frac{d y}{d x} \frac{d^2 y}{d x^2}-2 x\left(\frac{d y}{d x}\right)^2=4 \frac{d y}{d x} {/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \left(1-x^2\right) \frac{d^2 y}{d x^2}-x \frac{d y}{d x}=2 {/tex}

 2

OR
(ii)

If {tex}{y}^{{x}}={x}^{{y}}{/tex}, then find {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}}{/tex}.

Solution:

{tex}y^x=x^y \Rightarrow x \log y=y \log x{/tex}
Differentiating with respect to '{tex}x{/tex}',
{tex} \frac{x}{y} \frac{d y}{d x}+\log y=\frac{y}{x}+\frac{d y}{d x} \log x {/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{y}{x}\left(\frac{y-x \log y}{x-y \log x}\right) {/tex}

25.

Find the value of k so that the lines joining the points {tex}(1,-1,2){/tex} and {tex}(3,4, {k}){/tex} is perpendicular to the line joining the points {tex}(0,3,2){/tex} and {tex}(3,5,6){/tex}.

Solution:

The direction ratios of the two lines are: {tex}2,5, {k}-2{/tex} and {tex}3,2,4{/tex} respectively.
The lines are perpendicular.
{tex}\therefore 2(3)+5(2)+4(k-2){/tex}{tex}=0 \Rightarrow k=-2{/tex}

Section - C

This section comprises Short Answer (SA) type questions of 3 marks each.
26.

It is known that {tex}20 \%{/tex} of the students in a school have above {tex}90 \%{/tex} attendance and {tex}80 \%{/tex} of the students are irregular. Past year results show that {tex}80 \%{/tex} of students who have above {tex}90 \%{/tex} attendance and {tex}20 \%{/tex} of irregular students get 'A' grade in their annual examination. At the end of a year, a student is chosen at random from the school and is found to have an 'A' grade. What is the probability that the student is irregular?

Solution:

Let B : Student getting 'A' grade
{tex}{E}_1{/tex} : Student having above {tex}90 \%{/tex} attendance
{tex}{E}_2{/tex} : Student being irregular
{tex} P\left(E_1\right)= \frac{20}{100} ; {/tex}{tex}P\left(E_2\right)=\frac{80}{100} P\left(B \mid E_1\right){/tex}{tex}=\frac{80}{100} ; P\left(B \mid E_2\right)=\frac{20}{100} {/tex}
{tex} P\left(E_2 \mid A\right) =\frac{P\left(E_2\right) P\left(B \mid E_2\right)}{P\left(E_1\right) P\left(B \mid E_1\right)+P\left(E_2\right) P\left(B \mid E_2\right)} {/tex}
{tex} =\frac{0.8 \times 0.2}{0.2 \times 0.8+0.8 \times 0.2}=\frac{1}{2} {/tex}

27.(i)

If {tex}y=\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{1-x^{2}}{1+x^{2}}\right), 0 \lt x \lt 1{/tex}, then find {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex}.

Solution:

{tex}y=\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{1-x^2}{1+x^2}\right) {/tex}{tex}\text {, Put } x=\tan \theta \Rightarrow \theta=\tan ^{-1} x{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow y=\cos ^{-1}(\cos 2 \theta)={/tex}{tex}2 \theta=2 \tan ^{-1} x, {/tex}{tex}\therefore \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{2}{1+x^2}{/tex}

 3

OR
(ii)

If {tex}{x^y} = {e^{x - y}},{/tex} prove that {tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{\log x}}{{{{\left( {1 + \log x} \right)}^2}}}{/tex}

Solution:

We have {tex}{x^y} = {e^{x - y}}{/tex} 

Taking logarithm on both sides, we get

{tex}y\log x = x - y{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow y\left( {1 + \log x} \right) = x{/tex}

i.e. {tex}y = \frac{x}{{1 + \log x}}{/tex}

Differentiating both sides w.r.t. x, we get

{tex}\frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{{\left( {1 + \log x} \right).1 - x\left( {\frac{1}{x}} \right)}}{{{{\left( {1 + \log x} \right)}^2}}} = \frac{{\log x}}{{{{\left( {1 + \log x} \right)}^2}}}{/tex}

28.(i)

Find a matrix A such that {tex}{A}\left[\begin{array}{rr} 4 & 0 \\ -1 & -2 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{rr} 17 & 10 \\ 0 & -16 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
Also, find {tex}{A}^{-1}{/tex}.

Solution:

{tex} A =\left[\begin{array}{cc} 17 & 10 \\ 0 & -16 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} 4 & 0 \\ -1 & -2 \end{array}\right]^{-1} {/tex}
{tex} =-\frac{1}{8}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 17 & 10 \\ 0 & -16 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} -2 & 0 \\ 1 & 4 \end{array}\right] {/tex}
{tex} =\left[\begin{array}{cc} 3 & -5 \\ 2 & 8 \end{array}\right] {/tex}
Also, {tex}A^{-1}=\frac{1}{34}\left[\begin{array}{cc}8 & 5 \\ -2 & 3\end{array}\right]{/tex}

 3

OR
(ii)

Given a square matrix {tex}A{/tex} of order 3 such that {tex}A^{2}=\left[\begin{array}{rrr}3 & -4 & 4 \\ 0 & -1 & 0 \\ -2 & 2 & -3\end{array}\right]{/tex}, show that {tex}A^{3}=A^{-1}{/tex}.

Solution:

{tex}{A}^{{3}}={A}^{-1} \Leftrightarrow {A}^{4}={I}{/tex}
{tex}A^{4}=A^{2} A^{2}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 3 & -4 & 4 \\ 0 & -1 & 0 \\ -2 & 2 & -3 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 3 & -4 & 4 \\ 0 & -1 & 0 \\ -2 & 2 & -3 \end{array}\right]{/tex}{tex}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]=I{/tex}

29.

Find the particular solution of the differential equation:
{tex}{x} \cos {y} {dy}=\left({x} {e}^{{x}} \log {x}+{e}^{{x}}\right) {dx} {/tex}{tex}\text { given that } {y}=\frac{\pi}{2} \text { when } {x}=1{/tex}

Solution:

Separating the variables and integrating:
{tex} \int \cos y d y=\int e^x\left(\log x+\frac{1}{x}\right) d x {/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \sin y=e^x \log x+c {/tex}
Using {tex}y=\frac{\pi}{2}, x=1{/tex}, we get {tex}c=1{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} the particular solution is, {tex}\sin y=1+e^x \log x{/tex}

 3
30.

Show that the vectors {tex}3 \hat{i}+\hat{j}-2 \hat{k}, 2 \hat{i}-\hat{j}-8 \hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}4 \hat{i}-2 \hat{j}-7 \hat{k}{/tex} form the vertices of a right triangle.

Solution:

Let {tex}{A}(3 \hat{{i}}+\hat{{j}}-2 \hat{{k}}), {B}(2 \hat{{i}}-\hat{{j}}-8 \hat{{k}}){/tex}, and {tex}{C}(4 \hat{{i}}-2 \hat{{j}}-7 \hat{{k}}){/tex}, then {tex}\overrightarrow{A B}=-\hat{i}-2 \hat{j}-6 \hat{k}, {/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow{B C}=2 \hat{i}-\hat{j}+\hat{k}, {/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow{C A}=-\hat{i}+3 \hat{j}+5 \hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\overrightarrow{A B}+\overrightarrow{B C}+\overrightarrow{C A}={/tex}{tex}\overrightarrow{{CA}}=-2-3+5=0 {/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \overrightarrow{{BC}} \perp \overrightarrow{{CA}}, {/tex} {tex}\therefore {ABC}{/tex} is a right triangle.

 3
31.(i)

Find: {tex}\int \frac{{dx}}{\cos {x} \sqrt{\cos 2 {x}}}{/tex}

Solution:

{tex} \int \frac{d x}{\cos x \sqrt{\cos 2 x}} =\int \frac{d x}{\cos x \sqrt{\cos ^2 x-\sin ^2 x}} {/tex}
{tex} =\int \frac{\sec ^2 x}{\sqrt{1-\tan ^2 x}} d x {/tex}
{tex} =\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{1-t^2}} d t, \text { using } \tan x=t, \sec ^2 x \cdot d x=d t {/tex}
{tex} =\sin ^{-1} t+c=\sin ^{-1}(\tan x)+c {/tex}

 3

OR
(b)

Find: {tex}\int \frac{5 x-3}{\sqrt{1+4 x-2 x^{2}}} d x{/tex}

Solution:

{tex} \int \frac{5 x-3}{\sqrt{1+4 x-2 x^2}} d x {/tex}{tex}=-\frac{5}{4} \int \frac{-4 x+4}{\sqrt{1+4 x-2 x^2}} d x+\sqrt{2} \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{\left(\left(\sqrt{\frac{3}{2}}\right)^2-(x-1)^2\right)}} d x {/tex}
{tex} =-\frac{5}{2} \sqrt{1+4 x-2 x^2}{/tex}{tex}+\sqrt{2} \sin ^{-1}\left(\sqrt{\frac{2}{3}}(x-1)\right)+c {/tex}

32.

Check whether the relation {tex}S{/tex} in the set {tex}\mathbb{R}{/tex} of real numbers, defined as {tex}{S}=\left\{({a}, {b}): {a} \leq {b}^{2}\right\}{/tex} is reflexive, symmetric or transitive. Also, determine all {tex}x \in R{/tex} such that {tex}(x, x) \in S{/tex}.

Solution:

Reflexive: Let {tex}\frac{1}{2} \not \leq\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^2, {/tex} 
{tex}\therefore S{/tex} is not reflexive.
Symmetric: {tex}1 \not \leq\ 2^2 \Rightarrow(1,2) \not\in S{/tex} but {tex}2 \leq 1^2 \Rightarrow(2,1) \in S . {/tex} 
{tex}\therefore S{/tex} is not symmetric.
Transitive: {tex}10 \leq 6^2 \Rightarrow(10,6) \in S \& 6 \not\leq 3^2 {/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow(6,3) \not\in S{/tex} but {tex}10 \leq 3^2 \Rightarrow(10,3) \in S{/tex}
{tex}\therefore {S}{/tex} is not Transitive.
Let {tex}(x, x) \in S \Leftrightarrow x \leq x^2 \Leftrightarrow x \in R-({0}, 1){/tex}

Using integration, find the area of the region bounded by the curve {tex}y=\sqrt{4-x^{2}}{/tex}, the lines {tex}x=-\sqrt{2}{/tex} and {tex}x=\sqrt{3}{/tex} and the {tex}x{/tex}-axis.

Solution:


Area of the region bounded by the curve
{tex} =\int\limits_{-\sqrt{2}}^{\sqrt{3}} \sqrt{4-x^2} d x {/tex}
{tex} =\left[\frac{x}{2} \sqrt{4-x^2}+2 \sin ^{-1} \frac{x}{2}\right]_{-\sqrt{2}}^{\sqrt{3}} {/tex}
{tex} =\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}+2 \cdot \frac{\pi}{3}+1+2 \cdot \frac{\pi}{4}=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}+1+\frac{7 \pi}{6} {/tex}

Area of the region bounded by the curve
{tex} =\int\limits_1^3 \sqrt{{y}} {dy} {/tex}

{tex} {r} \vec{a}_2-\vec{a}_1=7 \hat{i}+38 \hat{j}-5 \hat{k}, \vec{b}_1 \times \vec{b}_2={/tex}
{tex}\left|\begin{array}{ccc} \hat{i} & \hat{j} & \hat{k} \\ 3 & -16 & 7 \\ 3 & 8 & -5 \end{array}\right|{/tex}
{tex}=24 \hat{i}+36 \hat{j}+72 \hat{k} {/tex}

Also, find the vector equation of the line passing through the point of intersection of the given lines and perpendicular to both the lines.

Solution:

If the given lines intersect then,
{tex} \hat{{i}}-\hat{{j}}+6 \hat{{k}}+\lambda(3 \hat{{i}}-\hat{{k}}){/tex}{tex}=-3 \hat{{j}}+3 \hat{{k}}+\mu(\hat{{i}}+2 \hat{{j}}-\hat{{k}}) {/tex}
Solving the equations, {tex}1+3 \lambda=\mu,-1=2 \mu-3{/tex}, we get {tex}\lambda=0, \mu=1{/tex}
which do not satisfy the equation, {tex}6-\lambda=3-\mu{/tex}.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} The lines do not intersect, hence no point of intersection

36.

An architect is developing a plot of land for a commercial complex. When asked about the dimensions of the plot, he said that if the length is decreased by 25 m and the breadth is increased by 25 m, then its area increases by {tex}625 {~m}^{2}{/tex}. If the length is decreased by 20 m and the breadth is increased by 10 m , then its area decreases by {tex}200 {~m}^{2}{/tex}.

On the basis of the above information, answer the following questions:

  1. Formulate the linear equations in x and y to represent the given information. (2)
  2. Find the dimensions of the plot of land by matrix method. (2)
Solution:
  1. {tex} (x-25)(y+25) =x y+625 \Rightarrow x-y=50 {/tex}
    {tex} (x-20)(y+10) =x y-200 \Rightarrow x-2 y=0 {/tex}
  2. The system of linear equations can be written in matrix form as
    {tex} {\left[\begin{array}{ll} 1 & -1 \\ 1 & -2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \end{array}\right] =\left[\begin{array}{c} 50 \\ 0 \end{array}\right] } ,{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ll}1 & -1 \\ 1 & -2\end{array}\right]^{-1}\left[\begin{array}{c}50 \\ 0\end{array}\right]{/tex}
    {tex}= -\left[\begin{array}{ll}-2 & 1 \\ -1 & 1\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c}50 \\ 0\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c}100 \\ 50\end{array}\right]{/tex}
    {tex} x=100 {~m}, y=50 {~m} {/tex}
